applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile (mode=default)
applied_optimizations.description: Wrapped PyTorch ConvTranspose3d with torch.compile(mode='default'). Correctness verified (5/5 trials pass). Speedup 0.996x.

attempted_but_failed.name: Manual Triton kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Timeout during compilation - kernel too complex with nested loops

notes: Reference Model signature: __init__(in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size, stride=1, padding=0, output_padding=0, groups=1, bias=False)
get_init_inputs() returns [32, 32, 3, 2, 1, 4] which maps to output_padding=4, groups=1 (default)
Despite problem description saying groups=4, the actual test uses groups=1 due to parameter ordering
Current torch.compile baseline is correct with speedup 0.996x (essentially same speed as reference)
Profiling shows 57.6% of time spent on data layout transforms (batched_transpose kernels)
Manual Triton kernel approach timed out - too complex for the time available
channels_last_3d and float16 optimizations made performance worse, not better

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4

global_notes: Stage 1 complete: baseline established at 2.28ms (reference), current best 2.29ms (torch.compile)
SCORE: 50.0 achieved (correct output, essentially same speed as baseline)
Main optimization opportunity: 57.6% time in data layout transforms could potentially be fused
